campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's financial health in July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and an update on the overall treasurer's report. The council also discussed plans to improve transportation options in Campbell by making it easier for residents to get around using different methods like buses, bikes, and cars.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and money management
Monthly financial report from the city treasurer
New multimodal transportation plan to improve how residents travel around Campbell

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to discuss a proposed new rule or law. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward with the ordinance and skipped the usual first reading step to speed up the process, with plans to officially vote on and approve it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input on proposed ordinance
Council waived first reading to accelerate the approval timeline
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption vote at next regular council meeting
Specific details of the ordinance not provided in this agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign contracts for construction services and professional agreements.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed February 2026 city finances and investments
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc.
Approved three-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 22, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a procedural meeting on April 22, 2026 to organize upcoming council sessions. They reviewed and finalized the agenda for their April 28 meeting, planned out the May 5 meeting agenda, and processed public records from the previous week. The council also addressed any scheduling needs and special requirements for agenda items.
keyTopics: Finalized April 28, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planned May 5, 2026 draft agenda
Assigned time slots for specific agenda items
Reviewed public records from April 9-16, 2026
Addressed waiver requirements for agenda documents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's city council met to discuss the budget and spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what construction and improvement projects the city wants to fund. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement budget and planned city projects
Recognition of Teen Month in Mountain View
Recognition of National Volunteer Week
Public input opportunity on city spending priorities

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a private closed-door meeting at 6 P.M. to discuss confidential matters, followed by a regular public meeting at 7 P.M. where they recognized some local winners and honored the flag. The specific details of what was discussed in the closed session weren't disclosed to the public.
keyTopics: Private closed-door council session (details not publicly disclosed)
Recognition of local winners/honorees
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 21, 2026, to discuss two legal cases where the city faces potential lawsuits. The council also recognized the work of the Administrative Services Department, which handles the city's budget, finances, and purchasing. Residents were given time to speak about any city issues they wanted to bring up.
keyTopics: Two pending lawsuits against the city
Recognition of Budget, Finance, and Purchasing divisions
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also approved a new contract with the local firefighters union covering 2026-2029. One item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Permission for oversized World Cup promotional signs around Levi's Stadium
New 3-year firefighters union contract approved
Limited-time allowance for building supergraphic advertisements
One agenda item pulled before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026, to approve the city's next fiscal year budget and fees, review tax policies, and discuss a county homelessness initiative. The council also approved previous meeting minutes and reviewed their upcoming calendar of meetings.
keyTopics: Approval of FY 2026-27 city budget and development fees
Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
County of Santa Clara Community Plan to End Homelessness
User fees updates and adjustments

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to reconsider a tree removal permit that was already approved for a property at 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to take another look at the decision to allow protected trees to be removed from this location.
keyTopics: Protected tree removal permit at 751 Waverley Street
Request for Director's Review of already-approved permit
Question whether tree removal decision should be reconsidered
